nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar leaned on longtime collaborators for DAMN., including jazz musician Terrace Martin
nuggets.text: While "HUMBLE." brought a new sound, Kendrick Lamar continued working with his core creative team on *DAMN.* Producer Terrace Martin, who crafted the lion's share of *To Pimp a Butterfly*, contributed to the track "Loyalty" on this album. Martin and Kendrick's creative process ensures they never repeat themselves, always striving for new musical moments.

source.title: Terrace Martin Speaks On How 'Black Messiah' Influenced Kendrick Lamar's 'TPAB' - Okayplayer
source.quoteSnippet: Los Angeles producer/musician Terrace Martin has been alongside Kendrick the whole time. He produced songs on each of those albums, including the lion’s share of To Pimp a Butterfly with Robert Glasper, Thundercat, and others. He only has one song on Damn—“Loyalty,” a silky collaboration with Rihanna—but it has the potential to be the album’s biggest hit.

nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar recorded "HUMBLE." first for DAMN. and dressed as the Pope in its video
nuggets.text: “HUMBLE." was the very first song Kendrick Lamar recorded for his album *DAMN.* Beyond the studio, its iconic music video, directed by Dave Meyers and The Little Homies, opens with Kendrick Lamar strikingly dressed in religious garb, reminiscent of the Pope. This visual choice immediately set a powerful tone for the song's challenging message.

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: "Humble" was the first song to be recorded for Damn... Directed by Dave Meyers and The Little Homies and released on March 30, 2017... the song's accompanying music video starts with Lamar dressed like the pope in a cope...

nuggets.headline: discover Black Hippy, the West Coast collective Kendrick Lamar helped form before his solo breakthrough
nuggets.text: If you're digging into Kendrick Lamar's roots, check out Black Hippy, the collective he formed in 2008 with fellow Top Dawg Entertainment labelmates Jay Rock, Ab-Soul, and ScHoolboy Q. This crew was dedicated to lyrical excellence and reinventing West Coast hip-hop, often bouncing ideas and guest features off each other's early projects.

source.title: The Handguide to Kendrick Lamar
source.quoteSnippet: In 2008, Lamar joined forces with TDE labelmates Jay Rock, Ab-Soul, and ScHoolboy Q to form the collective Black Hippy, a crew intent on lyrical excellence and reinventing West Coast hip-hop​. Together, they bounced ideas and guest features across each other’s projects, cultivating an alternative rap movement out of L.A.’s underbelly.

nuggets.imageCaption: Black Hippy members aimed to reinvent West Coast hip-hop.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar, born in Compton, transformed his early inspirations from Dr. Dre and Tupac into a Pulitzer Prize-winning career. Shedding his "K. Dot" alias, he emerged as a powerful, authentic voice, known for deeply introspective and socially conscious narratives in hip-hop.
